LAW AND MOTION 301, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT CONTINUED FROM MAR-03-2025 TO LAW AND MOTION AT APR-04-2025, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 301 ON COURTS OWN MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT WAS FILED ON JANUARY 13, 2025. ON FEBRUARY 11, 2025, PLAINTIFF SERVED A NOTICE OF RELATED CASE CONCERNING CHEMICAL TOXIN WORKING GROUP, INC. V. BUMBLE BEE FOODS LLC, L.A. SUPERIOR COURT NO. 23STCV17896. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RENOTICE ITS MOTION TO APPROVE THE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT FOR APRIL 4, 2025, AND TO SERVE THE PARTIES IN THE RELATED CASE WITH ITS MOVING PAPERS. PLAINTIFF IS FURTHER ORDERED TO SUBMIT A SHORT SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF NO LATER THAN 10 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE RENOTICED HEARING, SERVED ON THE PARTIES IN THE RELATED CASE AND THE ATTORNEY GENERAL ALONG WITH OPPOSING COUNSEL IN THIS CASE, EXPLAINING WHY THE PROPOSED PENALTY AMOUNT IS REASONABLE. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)."

The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature.

Order and consent Judgment are signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BUMBLE BEE FOODS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal

The court exercises its discretion to hear this unopposed motion on shortened time. The court grants the motion to seal permanently the portions of the Supplemental Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Further Support of Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and the Declaration of Evan Smith in Further Support of the Motion to Approve Consent Judgment that were previously lodged provisionally under seal. The court finds that the material ot be sealed contains information proprietary to defendant that is maintained confidentially by defendant. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-03-2025 Continued To Apr-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment

The court continues the matter on its own motion to April 4, 2025. Plaintiff's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement was filed on January 13, 2025. On February 11, 2025, Plaintiff served a Notice of Related Case concerning Chemical Toxin Working Group, Inc. v. Bumble Bee Foods LLC, L.A. Superior Court No. 23STCV17896. Plaintiff is ordered to renotice its motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement for April 4, 2025, and to serve the parties in the related case with its moving papers. Plaintiff is further ordered to submit a short supplemental brief no later than 10 court days before the renoticed hearing, served on the parties in the related case and the Attorney General along with opposing counsel in this case, explaining why the proposed penalty amount is reasonable.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
